name: isEmpty
description: The standalone lodash.isEmpty package as a vendorable file. Useful when you want lodash's emptiness checks without another npm dependency.
tagline: Check whether a value is empty by lodash rules.
summary: This page ships the published lodash.isEmpty file in both raw and normalized forms. The value is to keep lodash's exact emptiness semantics local and inspectable.
nativeAlternative: Simple length or key-count checks only cover subsets of cases; lodash's emptiness rules across value types are wider than a one-liner.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
